title: Chicken Cordon Bleu II
description: 'Cordon Bleu' is a French term, literally translated as 'blue ribbon', that originally referred to an award for culinary excellence given to women cooks! The term can now apply to any superior cook (yes, men too), and also to this dish (chicken, ham and Swiss cheese slices, breaded and sauteed). This yummy version adds paprika and a creamy white wine sauce worthy of its own blue ribbon. Two blue ribbon tastes in wedded bliss -- Chicken Cordon Bleu II!

directions.displayValue: Pound chicken breasts if they are too thick. Place a cheese and ham slice on each breast within 1/2 inch of the edges. Fold the edges of the chicken over the filling, and secure with toothpicks. Mix the flour and paprika in a small bowl, and coat the chicken pieces.

Heat the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat, and cook the chicken until browned on all sides. Add the wine and bouillon. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, until chicken is no longer pink and juices run clear.

Remove the toothpicks, and transfer the breasts to a warm platter. Blend the cornstarch with the cream in a small bowl, and whisk slowly into the skillet. Cook, stirring until thickened, and pour over the chicken. Serve warm.

topReviews.text: This is very good -- but I'd recommend a few changes.  First, pound the breasts to half-inch thickness at least (this will let you reduce the 30 min cook time to about 15, max).  Then, reduce fat a little by using half butter and half olive oil when you brown (3 tbsp. of each).  Oil will keep the butter from burning, too.  Third, if you want to eliminate wine, just use 1/2 cup chicken broth (from a can, if needed) -- or just use salted water.  No big deal.  Last, when I blend the cream at the last stage, I like to add about 1/4 cup fresh chopped basil to the mix -- serve with chopped fresh basil on top, too - or substitute whatever herb you like (fresh is best, but only add it last).

I made a few changes to this recipe. First, I brushed the chicken with egg and rolled in breadcrumbs instead of flour. Second, I fried the chicken in butter for about 10 minutes until it was nicely browned, then I put it in the oven at 350 for another 20 minutes. Instead of simmering the chicken in the sauce, I whisked the cream,wine, bouillon and cornstarch in a bowl, then simmered it for about 10 min. alone, and spooned it over the chicken.
It turned out fantastic. My fiancee` said this was the best dish I ever made, and he's more of a beef man. Thank you!

I have to add my 5 stars to this one.  I scaled this recipe to 24 for a holiday dinner.  All ages LOVED this meal served with rice pilaf and roasted asparagus and quartered bella mushroomsi. I recommend an oven roasted, no water added ham, and a good quality wine.  After making this again recently, I learned that even a small chicken breast, after pounded and filled, is too large a portion.  My 6 adult guests could only eat half of theirs.  Going forward, I will slice this into 1 inch rounds (much prettier, too) as pictured in photo number 26.  Thanks, Behr.

recipes.title: Skillet Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Mixing the chicken, ham and cheese together eliminates the assembly time of the usual stuffed chicken rolls, and a wine and soup sauce bind it all together.

recipes.title: Different Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: This casserole still features ham and Swiss cheese with chicken, but strays from the original with a sauce of cream soup, sour cream and wine. Stuffing mix tops the entire creation.

recipes.title: Chicken Cordon Bleu I
recipes.description: A delicious French classic, chicken cordon bleu is made of chicken breasts stuffed with ham and Swiss cheese.

recipes.title: Cordon Bleu Chicken Rolls
recipes.description: Breaded chicken stuffed with ham and cheese sounds pretty pedestrian, but it tastes extraordinary. This recipe includes an easy cream sauce to boot.

recipes.title: Healthier Chicken Cordon Bleu II
recipes.description: A classic dish made healthier by using half and half instead of heavy cream and lemon juice instead of white wine. This version of Chicken Cordon Bleu is still ribbon worthy.

recipes.title: Easy Baked Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Easy recipe that tastes like you cooked all day. Chicken breasts are wrapped around ham and mozzarella cheese for a change in this version of the classic baked dish.

recipes.title: Weeknight Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Chicken breasts are wrapped with sliced ham and Swiss cheese, and baked in a creamy chicken soup sauce.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Layers of chicken, ham and Swiss cheese simmer in a creamy sauce while topped with stuffing mix.

recipes.title: Saucy Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: You take your basic Chicken Cordon Bleu--breaded chicken with ham and Swiss cheese--and jazz it up with a mushroom-sour cream sauce with a hint of curry and wine.

recipes.title: Chicken Cordon Bleu a la Kerry
recipes.description: This flavorful spin on chicken cordon bleu uses coppa and white Cheddar cheese, and is topped with a rich and creamy wine sauce.

recipes.title: Chicken Cordon Bleu I
recipes.description: A delicious French classic, chicken cordon bleu is made of chicken breasts stuffed with ham and Swiss cheese.

recipes.title: Cordon Bleu Chicken Rolls
recipes.description: Breaded chicken stuffed with ham and cheese sounds pretty pedestrian, but it tastes extraordinary. This recipe includes an easy cream sauce to boot.

recipes.title: Easy Baked Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Easy recipe that tastes like you cooked all day. Chicken breasts are wrapped around ham and mozzarella cheese for a change in this version of the classic baked dish.

recipes.title: Weeknight Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipes.description: Chicken breasts are wrapped with sliced ham and Swiss cheese, and baked in a creamy chicken soup sauce.

recipes.title: Chicken Marsala
recipes.description: Herbed chicken in a sweet Marsala and mushroom sauce -- sounds simple, and it is -- simply delicious.

recipes.title: Baked Teriyaki Chicken
recipes.description: A spicy, homemade teriyaki of soy sauce, cider vinegar, ginger and garlic enlivens chicken thighs or pieces. Easy to double for a large group.

recipes.title: Roast Sticky Chicken-Rotisserie Style
recipes.description: Ever wish you could get that restaurant-style rotisserie chicken at home? Well, with minimal preparation and about 5 hours' cooking time, you can!

recipes.title: Rosemary Ranch Chicken Kabobs
recipes.description: This rosemary ranch chicken recipe is so delicious, tender, and juicy the chicken will melt in your mouth.

recipes.title: Spinach Stuffed Chicken Breasts
recipes.description: Tangy feta cheese and spinach make a zesty stuffing for chicken wrapped with bacon.

recipes.title: Baked Pork Chops I
recipes.description: Pork chops are coated with seasoned Italian crumbs and baked with an easy mushroom and wine sauce for for a nice weeknight dinner that practically takes care of itself.

footnotes.text: Aluminum foil can be used to keep food moist, cook it evenly, and make clean-up easier.
